title: Heilbronn (district)

infobox.value: (German pronunciation: [[haɪlˈbʁɔn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-Heilbronn.ogg "File:De-Heilbronn.ogg")) is a [*Landkreis* (district)](/wiki/Districts_of_Germany "Districts of Germany") in the north of [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Neighboring districts are (from north clockwise) [Neckar-Odenwald](/wiki/Neckar-Odenwald "Neckar-Odenwald"), [Hohenlohe](/wiki/Hohenlohe_(district) "Hohenlohe (district)"), [Schwäbisch Hall](/wiki/Schw%C3%A4bisch_Hall_(district) "Schwäbisch Hall (district)"), [Rems-Murr](/wiki/Rems-Murr "Rems-Murr"), [Ludwigsburg](/wiki/Ludwigsburg_(district) "Ludwigsburg (district)"), [Enz](/wiki/Enz_(district) "Enz (district)"), [Karlsruhe](/wiki/Karlsruhe_(district) "Karlsruhe (district)") and [Rhein-Neckar](/wiki/Rhein-Neckar "Rhein-Neckar"). In the centre of it is the free-city of [Heilbronn](/wiki/Heilbronn "Heilbronn"), which is its own separate administrative area.

["Tabellengruppe 12411: Fortschreibung des Bevölkerungsstandes zum 31. Dezember 2024"](https://daten.statistik-bw.de/genesisonline/online?operation=statistic&levelindex=0&levelid=1757432774350&code=12411#abreadcrumb) [Update of the population as of 31 December 2024] (in German). **Landkreis Heilbronn** (German pronunciation: [[haɪlˈbʁɔn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-Heilbronn.ogg "File:De-Heilbronn.ogg")) is a [*Landkreis* (district)](/wiki/Districts_of_Germany "Districts of Germany") in the north of [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Neighboring districts are (from north clockwise) [Neckar-Odenwald](/wiki/Neckar-Odenwald "Neckar-Odenwald"), [Hohenlohe](/wiki/Hohenlohe_(district) "Hohenlohe (district)"), [Schwäbisch Hall](/wiki/Schw%C3%A4bisch_Hall_(district) "Schwäbisch Hall (district)"), [Rems-Murr](/wiki/Rems-Murr "Rems-Murr"), [Ludwigsburg](/wiki/Ludwigsburg_(district) "Ludwigsburg (district)"), [Enz](/wiki/Enz_(district) "Enz (district)"), [Karlsruhe](/wiki/Karlsruhe_(district) "Karlsruhe (district)") and [Rhein-Neckar](/wiki/Rhein-Neckar "Rhein-Neckar"). In the centre of it is the free-city of [Heilbronn](/wiki/Heilbronn "Heilbronn"), which is its own separate administrative area.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heilbronn_(district)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The predecessor to the district is the *Oberamt Heilbronn*, which was created in 1803 when the previously Free Imperial City of Heilbronn was incorporated into the [Electorate of Württemberg](/wiki/Electorate_of_W%C3%BCrttemberg "Electorate of Württemberg"). In 1926, about half of the Oberamt (old district) of Weinsberg was added. In 1938, it was recognized as a district, and in addition to the previous Oberamt, parts of the dissolved Oberämter Neckarsulm, Brackenheim, Marbach and Besigheim were added. The city of Heilbronn was not included in the district. In 1973, the *Landkreise* (districts) were reorganized, and part of the dissolved districts of Sinsheim, Mosbach, Buchen and Schwäbisch Hall were added. Within the following two years 5 municipalities were incorporated into the city and therefore left the district, which got its current borders in 1975.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heilbronn_(district)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

The main river in the district is the [Neckar](/wiki/Neckar "Neckar"), which flows through the district from the south to the north. The western part of the district belongs to the landscape *Kraichgau*, the east to the *Hohenloher Ebene*, *Kocher-Jagst-Ebene*, and the *Löwensteiner Berge*.

## Coat of arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heilbronn_(district)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Coat of arms")]

The coat of arms shows a clawless eagle, the symbol of the Counts of Lauffen, who ruled the area of the district in the 13th century.
